methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $8M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Cache Energy, a Seed-stage company in Climate & Energy founded by Arpit Dwivedi, has raised $8.5M to date. The sector includes 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers spanning companies like Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to data constraints. Stage-progression triangulation, using the $8.5M raised as a proxy base value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to the next funding round, implies a valuation of $21M. A peer-median approach was not viable: while 9 same-sector peers exist in the Climate & Energy universe, none satisfied the minimum threshold of three datapoints when filtered by stage proximity or comparable valuation tier, making a narrow peer set comparison unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $18M (low) to $24M (high), with a median point of $21M. The range reflects typical variance around stage-progression assumptions. Key uncertainties stem from the reliance on a single method and the absence of direct stage-comparable peers. Resolution would require either near-term funding round outcomes from similar Climate & Energy seed companies or expanded visibility into the private peer set's post-money valuations and stage distributions.
